footer.disclaimer: {title} is not affiliated with the Kodokan or the IJF. All judo techniques mentioned belong to judo heritage and are catalogued according to the official Kodokan nomenclature.
tech.page_description: Discover all 100 official Kodokan Judo techniques: throws, pins, chokes, and joint locks.
tech.intro: The Kodokan officially recognizes 100 judo techniques, divided into two families: throwing techniques (Nage-waza) and grappling techniques (Katame-waza). Explore each one below.
tech.nage_intro: Throwing techniques (Nage-waza) are performed standing and aim to throw the opponent to the ground.
tech.katame_intro: Grappling techniques (Katame-waza) include pins, chokes, and joint locks performed on the ground.
tech.meta_desc: {name} ({translation}) is a judo technique from the {group} group. Discover its characteristics and related techniques.
tech.is_a: {name} ({translation}) is a {category} technique belonging to the {group} group.
tech.performed: It is performed from a {position} position.
